_comment: Tier-2 Layer-C: NGSolve Stokes Taylor-Hood MMS — cross-validates fenics + skfem stokes. Same MMS: ψ=sin(πx)²·sin(πy)², u=curl(ψ), p=sin(πx)·cos(πy), ν=1, f=-ν·Δu+grad(p). Distinct NGSolve API: Mesh(unit_square.GenerateMesh(maxh)), VectorH1*H1 compound, BilinearForm with InnerProduct(Grad(u),Grad(v)) + div(u)·q + div(v)·p (POSITIVE-divergence convention — opposite sign to fenics, so p_h = -p_exact in canonical convention; fixture negates p_h before computing error), Pardiso/MUMPS/UMFPACK fallback chain, single-DOF pressure pin via free.Clear(V.ndof) (Pardiso phase-33 error -4 without pinning). Asserts P2 u L2 <= 1.5e-3, P1 p L2 <= 1e-2, u EOC in [2.0, 4.0], p EOC in [1.3, 4.0] (Netgen unstructured mesh super-converges more than Cartesian; fenics/skfem p EOC ≈2.63 on Cartesian, NGSolve ≈3.48 on Netgen). Caught two implementation bugs during development: (1) Pardiso singular system without pressure pin → fixed via free.Clear; (2) opposite sign convention → fixed by negating p_h. Both bugs are catalog pitfalls worth recording. Verified on 2026-06-01: u L2=7.70e-4, p L2=3.71e-3, u EOC=3.280, p EOC=3.481.
